episodes.title: Jim Jones' Son Tells the Inside Story of the Peoples Temple Cult | Interview with Jim Jones Jr.
episodes.description:
What was it like growing up inside the Peoples Temple — and witnessing its tragic end?

Official website: https://www.pbs.org/wgbh/americanexperience/ | #AmericanExperiencePBS

In this interview from the American Experience archives, Jim Jones Jr., son of Peoples Temple leader Jim Jones, reflects on his experiences growing up and the events that culminated in the Jonestown massacre, the 1978 tragedy in Guyana where more than 900 people died in the largest mass murder-suicide in history. Through rarely heard anecdotes, he offers a deeply personal perspective on life inside his father's church, the ideals of social justice that drew people in, and the devastating consequences that followed.

Jones Jr. spoke to filmmaker Stanley Nelson on May 28, 2005. This interview was conducted for the 2007 American Experience documentary JONESTOWN: THE LIFE AND DEATH OF PEOPLES TEMPLE and is being published as part of our series spotlighting remarkable archival conversations with historians, journalists, eyewitnesses, and other primary sources whose insights deepen our understanding of the past. This interview has been edited for length and clarity.

Learn more about JONESTOWN: THE LIFE AND DEATH OF PEOPLES TEMPLE: https://www.pbs.org/wgbh/americanexperience/films/jonestown/

52:09 Living in Jonestown, Guyana
57:37 John Victor Stoen: The Legal Battle that Sparked Tragedy
1:01:21 Surviving November 18, 1978
1:13:16 Unspoken Racial Divides at Jonestown
